name: Pet Crimes
white: A cat slowly pushing your cup off the counter while making direct eye contact.
The dog's secret second family down the street, who call him "Buster."
A hamster making its getaway at one mile per hour in a plastic ball.
The goldfish, who saw everything and will say nothing.
A parrot taking the witness stand.
A dog who ate the evidence.
A cat burglar who turns out to be an actual cat.
The 3 a.m. zoomies setting off the burglar alarm.
A dog doing the slow, guilty walk past the shredded couch cushion.
A lizard who escaped three years ago and lives in the walls now.
Every pet in the house on the kitchen counter the second the car leaves the driveway.
A heist crew of one dog and one raccoon.
A cat carefully planting the evidence in the dog's bed.
A dog tunneling under the fence toward the smell of hamburgers.
A police sketch of a very good boy.
A lineup of five identical orange cats.
The cat's alibi, which does not hold up.
A dog with forty-one stolen socks hidden under the porch.
A hallway of shredded toilet paper and one extremely proud dog.
A getaway turtle, still crossing the driveway.
A kitten with a face too cute to convict.
A "MISSING" poster for a cat that is sitting right there.
A dog holding Dad's left shoe for ransom.
A ferret emptying your backpack one shiny thing at a time.
Two cats in a trench coat buying dog treats.
Saying "we're keeping him" for the ninth time this year.
A house where every blanket has a cat under it.
The third hamster named Steve.
A goldfish tank that started with one goldfish and now has its own zip code.
A rescue mission that came home with four extra cats.
